name: command_run
description: Run tools as a pure batch+step command runner.

commands.description: Executable command-line instructions. During task execution, complete all currently needed steps in one batch; prefer 5 or more commands. Include only commands whose inputs are already known when this batch is created. Independent read/search commands may share one step. Do not create test/probe commands that depend on output from earlier commands in the same batch.

command_type.description: Target internal command name from the available commands list. For namespaced tools use the flattened name shown there exactly.

command_line.description: String payload for the target command. For JSON-schema commands, pass an escaped JSON object string such as {"cmd":"rg -n query src"}. For freeform commands such as apply_patch, pass the raw freeform body. For shell-like commands, plain text is also accepted and mapped to that tool's command field.

step.description: Execution order and dependency group. Commands in the same step may run together when they do not depend on each other's output. Use a later step only when that command depends on earlier known work or must run after edits. Duplicate or earlier step values are extended into later unique steps in input order. When using compact_context, it must be the final command in the highest step.
